rationale: Both cover the two resolved inline concerns (regression tests, CuMemTag enum) similarly, but Y's headline blocker — that the unconditional level-2 NotImplementedError guard breaks five specific pre-existing diffusion tests (with file:line evidence verifiable against the diff's own hunk context) — is precisely the over-strictness that historically broke CI post-merge (#4905/#4912), directly hitting the latent gap. X's related 'bad default level=2' finding is more speculative/design-opinion and never checks whether existing tests/CI actually exercise the guarded path. Y's findings (docs example now broken, dead `_is_sleeping` test helper) are also concretely grounded via grep, edging out X on precision despite similar actionability.
